title: Profile icon account creation flow with user dashboard stats

requirements.text: When the user clicks the profile icon in HubTopBar, if the user does not have an existing profile, guide them through the account creation flow (Internet Identity authentication + Google Sign-In for Gmail linking + profile details form) before granting access to the profile view. Only after a profile is successfully created or confirmed to exist should the dashboard be displayed.

quotes: When the user clicks on the profile icon, let them create their account first, then show those dashboards

acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the profile icon when no profile exists initiates the account creation / onboarding flow
The user cannot access the dashboard until they have completed account creation
Clicking the profile icon when a profile already exists goes directly to the dashboard
The onboarding flow includes Internet Identity login, optional Google Sign-In for Gmail linking, and profile details entry
On successful profile creation, the user is transitioned to the dashboard view

requirements.text: Add a user dashboard section within ProfileView that displays the following stat cards for the authenticated user: (1) Tasks Completed — total number of tasks the user has completed as a performer, (2) User Rating — the user's average star rating received from task creators, (3) Total Ratings — the total number of rating reviews the user has received, and (4) Tasks Posted — total number of tasks the user has created. Fetch this data from the user's backend Profile record.

quotes: show those dashboards of task completed, user ratings, total ratings, and all those

acceptanceCriteria: The dashboard displays a 'Tasks Completed' stat card with the correct count
The dashboard displays a 'User Rating' stat card showing the average star rating (e.g., 4.5 ★)
The dashboard displays a 'Total Ratings' stat card with the total number of ratings received
The dashboard displays a 'Tasks Posted' stat card with the correct count
All stats are loaded from the backend Profile data for the authenticated user
A loading skeleton is shown while data is being fetched
Stat cards use the existing PROXIIS neon/dark visual theme with the green-to-purple gradient accents

requirements.text: Ensure the backend Profile type and related query endpoints expose all necessary fields for the dashboard stats: tasksCompleted count, averageRating, totalRatingsCount, and tasksPosted count. Add or update these fields in the Motoko actor if they are not already present or not returned by the existing getProfile/getCallerProfile endpoints.

quotes: task completed, user ratings, total ratings

acceptanceCriteria: The Profile type includes tasksCompleted, averageRating, totalRatingsReceived, and tasksPosted fields
The getCallerProfile (or equivalent) endpoint returns these fields populated with correct values
Existing profile creation and update logic initializes these fields to zero/empty defaults
Rating and completion counts are updated correctly by existing task completion and verification mutations

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
All Motoko logic must remain in the single main actor file (backend/main.mo)
Preserve the existing PROXIIS neon grid / dark theme visual identity
nonGoals: Redesigning the landing page or hub view
Adding new task categories or task management features
Payment or QR code flow changes
